title: Retrospective Validation Fixture Corpus
description: Evidence Foundry E1 Phase 4 (FR-19/FR-20, ADR-0006). Structural de-identification boundary for the retrospective validation harness (tools/retro-validate/) -- TOOL-LOCAL BY DESIGN so no wave-2 phase touches scripts/validate-kb.mjs. E1 corpus content is restricted to synthetic + de-identified fixtures ONLY: every case MUST carry a `provenance` marker in {synthetic, deidentified} and the corpus document MUST carry a corpus-level `sourceAttestation` reference. This schema structurally FORBIDS identifier fields at the case envelope -- an enumerated denylist covering name, MRN (medical record number), DOB (date of birth), address, and contact fields is enforced by the closed `case` property set (`additionalProperties: false`: none of those keys are ever admitted, by omission, not by a hand-maintained blocklist keyword), plus a distinct SSN-like-value-pattern denylist (`not`/`pattern`) applied to every free-text case identifier/reference string, so a disguised identifier cannot ride along inside an otherwise-permitted field. Real (non-synthetic, non-deidentified) patient data can never satisfy this schema -- there is no `provenance` value for it. A corpus validating against this schema is a STRUCTURAL claim only: it proves the fixture-corpus shape and the de-identification boundary held. It is never a clinical-validity, safety, diagnostic-performance, or IRB/DUA-compliance claim, and it never certifies the underlying real-world de-identification process (if any) that produced a `deidentified` case upstream of this repo -- that is a human/governance act (gate G3, SPIKE-007), not something this schema, or any software, can attest to.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer fixture-corpus-document-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes.

corpusId.description: Stable slug identifying this corpus (e.g. in run-provenance.json / access-log.jsonl corpus-level references, FR-21/FR-22). Never a patient identifier.

description.description: Free-text human summary of what this corpus covers. Authors MUST NOT place any patient-identifying detail here -- this field is not schema-checked against the identifier denylist (unstructured prose cannot be), so it is a documented human obligation, not a structural guarantee.

cases.description: Every element MUST validate against `$defs/case`, in particular its `provenance` requirement and closed identifier-free property set.

sourceAttestation.description: Corpus-level source attestation (FR-20): where this corpus's content came from, at a reference-only granularity. `ref` is a citation/pointer (e.g. a synthetic-generation note id, or an external partner's DUA/IRB document id per SPIKE-007) -- NEVER patient-identifying content itself.

ref.description: Attestation reference/citation id. Structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value (###-##-####).

provenanceClass.description: Corpus-wide provenance class. E1 admits no other value -- there is no 'real'/'identified' option.

deidentificationStandard.description: Populated only when `provenanceClass` is `deidentified`; the named de-identification standard (HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination) the upstream, out-of-repo process claims to have followed. This field records a CLAIM about upstream process, not a guarantee this schema enforces or can enforce.

case.description: One fixture case. Closed property set (`additionalProperties: false`): `name`, `patientName`, `mrn`, `medicalRecordNumber`, `dob`, `dateOfBirth`, `address`, `contact`, `contactInfo`, `phone`, `email`, `ssn`, `socialSecurityNumber`, or any other identifier-shaped key is rejected by omission -- it is simply not in the allowed key set below, not by a hand-maintained negative list. `caseId`/`sourceRef` additionally reject any SSN-like value pattern, independent of key name.

caseId.description: Stable case identifier local to this corpus. Never a patient identifier -- structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value.

provenance.description: REQUIRED per-case provenance marker (FR-20). No 'real'/'identified' value exists in this enum -- there is no way to mark a case as real patient data and have it validate.

sourceRef.description: Optional pointer into the corpus-level `sourceAttestation` (e.g. a sub-batch id). Structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value.

tags.description: Free-form regression-lane tags (e.g. `dangerous-miss-regression` for the P4-T8 E0 adapter output).

clinicalInput.description: Deterministic clinical facts only -- mirrors the closed top-level key set of schemas/patient-input.schema.json (itself already additionalProperties:false, no free-text identifier fields, no PHI by construction). Any key outside this exact set -- in particular any direct or indirect identifier field -- is structurally rejected here, one level before the harness ever reaches the engine's own patient-input validation (P4-T3). Sub-shape depth is intentionally NOT re-validated here (that duplication belongs to the engine's own schema, not this tool-local boundary schema) -- FR-20's job is the identifier denylist, not a second copy of the full clinical schema.

referenceLabels.description: Human-authored expected-output labels this case will be compared against for software-agreement metrics (FR-21, OQ-5) -- not present until a corpus author supplies them; entirely optional at the schema level.
